sources.issuing_organisation: Department of Health – Abu Dhabi; Abu Dhabi Public Health Centre
sources.version: V10; DOH/WCV/SD/10/2025; revised standard; document states effective date TBD
sources.population: Children aged 1 month to 6 years attending well-child visit screening services.
sources.clinical_setting: DoH-licensed healthcare facilities providing well-child visit screening services in Abu Dhabi.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for age-specific well-child screening in Abu Dhabi; partial for children outside the stated age range and for symptom-led growth or developmental presentations. The published copy states an effective date of TBD.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Standard Scope; 3.1 History Components; 3.2 Comprehensive Physical Examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the 1-month-to-6-year population and requires parent history, age-specific physical examination, vital signs, developmental milestones, screening, vaccination discussion, and EMR documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — Child Screening Requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies age-linked weight, length or height, head circumference, growth plotting, feeding and medical history, developmental milestones, screening, immunization review, and caregiver counselling.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 5 — Developmental Delays Screening; Appendix 6 — Normal Developmental Milestones
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines developmental and autism screening prompts, parent concern, walking, speech and social domains, and referral pathways for concerning screening results.

exact_sections.heading: 4. Key Stakeholder Roles and Responsibilities
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires effective record keeping and documentation of screening findings, caregiver education, consent, and refusal.
